title: Nokia Launches Agentic AI for Networks
optimized_headline: Nokia Unveils Agentic AI: A Game Changer for Network Management
raw_excerpt: The vendor is rolling out AI agents across its fixed network platforms to automate troubleshooting, improve customer support and accelerate fiber deployment.
raw_body: The vendor is rolling out AI agents across its fixed network platforms to automate troubleshooting, improve customer support and accelerate fiber deployment.
speedrun: Nokia has introduced Agentic AI to enhance its fixed network platforms. This technology aims to automate troubleshooting and improve customer support, which could speed up fiber deployment. By leveraging AI agents, Nokia hopes to streamline operations and deliver better services. This move is significant as it reflects the growing trend of using AI to optimize network management and customer interactions.
why_it_matters: Telecom companies could see faster issue resolution, leading to improved customer satisfaction and reduced operational costs.
This development indicates a broader shift in the telecommunications industry towards automation, potentially reshaping how networks are managed and services delivered.

lenses.eli12: Nokia's new AI technology acts like a smart assistant for networks. Just as a personal assistant helps manage your schedule, these AI agents troubleshoot issues and support customers. This is important because it could lead to quicker solutions and better service for everyone using the network.
lenses.pm: For product managers, Nokia's Agentic AI highlights a growing user need for efficient customer support and network reliability. Automating troubleshooting could lower costs and improve service speed. This means companies might focus more on innovation rather than manual problem-solving.
lenses.engineer: Nokia's Agentic AI utilizes advanced algorithms to automate network troubleshooting and support tasks. By integrating AI agents, the system could enhance operational efficiency and reduce downtime in fiber deployment. However, engineers should consider the challenges of integrating AI into existing infrastructure.
